name: Hand
description: The hand is composed of 27 bones
The carpal bones are arranged in two rows.
The proximal row includes the scaphoid, lunate, triquetrum, and pisiform.
The distal row includes the trapezium, trapezoid, capitate, and hamate.
The scaphoid, lunate, and triquetrum bones articulate with the radius and articular disc of the radio-ulnar joint.
* A mnemonic for remembering the carpal bones is “Scary Lunatics Try Poisons That They Can’t Handle.
The first letter of each word is the first letter of each of the bones from lateral to medial, proximal row first.
